topic: AGENTS.md Guide 2026: How to Write AI Agent Instructions for Every Tool

competitors.title: The Complete Guide to AGENTS.md for AI Coding Agents (2026)
key_points: AGENTS.md is a cross-tool standard markdown file at repo root for agent instructions, stewarded by Agentic AI Foundation under Linux Foundation
60,000+ GitHub repos use it; 20+ compatible coding agents including Codex, Cursor, Claude Code, Windsurf, Devin
Princeton study: 28.6% median runtime reduction, 16.6% fewer tokens with human-written AGENTS.md (98.6s → 70.3s, 2,925 → 2,440 tokens)
LLM-generated AGENTS.md files reduced success by 2% and increased cost by 23%; human-written improved success ~4%
Six key content categories: Build & Test Commands, Code Style Rules, Project Structure, Testing Instructions, Git Workflow, Boundaries
Key anti-patterns: vague instructions, duplicating README content, overly long files
Codex enforces 32 KiB default size cap; supports directory hierarchy with nested AGENTS.md files (nearest takes precedence)
OpenAI Codex repo uses 88 AGENTS.md files across its monorepo structure

competitors.title: How to Configure Every AI Coding Assistant: CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, Cursor Rules and More
key_points: Complete landscape of AI config files: CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, GEMINI.md, .cursorrules/.cursor/rules/, copilot-instructions.md, .windsurfrules
AGENTS.md is the closest thing to a universal standard, now stewarded by Linux Foundation with 60,000+ open-source projects
Claude Code reads AGENTS.md as fallback when no CLAUDE.md exists — enabling cross-tool compatibility
Frontier LLMs can reliably follow ~150-200 instructions; Claude Code's system prompt uses ~50, so keep AGENTS.md under 300 lines
Most common mistake: treating config files like comprehensive documentation rather than concise actionable instructions
Best approach: start with AGENTS.md as single source of truth, add tool-specific files only for unique features
Key anti-patterns: writing novels (over 500 lines), duplicating across tools, using /init auto-generators, including things a linter handles
Recommended structure: Commands → Tech Stack → Project Structure → Conventions → Boundaries

competitors.title: AGENTS.md: The Open Standard for Guiding AI Coding Agents (2026 Guide)
key_points: AGENTS.md is an open standard backed by Agentic AI Foundation (AAIF) under Linux Foundation, alongside MCP and Goose
Compatible with: Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Cursor, Windsurf, Devin, Gemini CLI, Aider, VS Code AI, and more
Seven recommended sections: Project Overview, Development Environment, Codebase Structure, Code Style & Conventions, Testing, PR & Commit Guidelines, Security Considerations
Development Environment section is the most critical — build commands are what AI agents use most frequently
Best practices: keep it actionable (not vague), update regularly, use monorepo-aware placement, don't duplicate README
For monorepos, place AGENTS.md at multiple levels — AI agents read nearest file in directory tree
AGENTS.md and README.md serve different audiences: README for humans, AGENTS.md for AI agents
Free AGENTS.md generator tool available with 6 presets for popular stacks (React, Next.js, Python, Node, Go, Rust)

competitors.title: New Research Reassesses the Value of AGENTS.md Files for AI Coding
key_points: ETH Zurich study (Feb 2026) tested 4 agents (Claude 3.5 Sonnet, Codex GPT-5.2, GPT-5.1 mini, Qwen Code) on 138 real-world Python tasks
LLM-generated AGENTS.md reduced task success by 3% and increased inference costs by 20%+
Human-written AGENTS.md increased task success by 4% on average but raised costs by up to 19%
Agents followed AGENTS.md instructions but ran more tests, read more files, did more grep searches — thorough but often unnecessary
Context files don't function as effective repository overviews — agents still spend time locating files
Researchers recommend omitting LLM-generated files entirely and limiting human-written files to non-inferable details (specific tooling, custom build commands)
Developer pushback: '4% gains on mixed-quality public projects suggests much higher gains for private enterprise projects with high-quality AGENTS files'
The study used niche repos (deliberately avoiding SWE-bench bias) — may not reflect typical enterprise usage patterns

stats.fact: 60,000+ open-source repositories now contain AGENTS.md files
stats.source: agents.md official site / ETH Zurich study

stats.fact: AGENTS.md supported by 20+ AI coding agents including Codex, Cursor, Claude Code, Windsurf, Devin, Gemini CLI, Aider
Princeton study: Human-written AGENTS.md reduced runtime 28.6% (98.6s → 70.3s) and tokens 16.6% (2,925 → 2,440)
ETH Zurich study: LLM-generated AGENTS.md reduced success rate by 3% and increased costs by 20%+
ETH Zurich study: Human-written AGENTS.md improved success rate by 4% but raised costs by up to 19%
OpenAI Codex repository uses 88 AGENTS.md files across its monorepo structure
Codex CLI enforces 32 KiB default size cap on AGENTS.md files
Frontier LLMs can reliably follow ~150-200 instructions; Claude Code system prompt uses ~50, leaving room for ~100-150 user instructions
AGENTS.md stewarded by Agentic AI Foundation (AAIF) under Linux Foundation, alongside MCP (Anthropic) and Goose (Block)
Windsurf enforces 6,000 character limit per rule file and 12,000 character total across all rules
GitHub Copilot supports scoped instructions via .github/instructions/*.instructions.md with glob-pattern frontmatter (since July 2025)
Agentic AI market projected to reach $10.86 billion in 2026, with 62% of organizations experimenting and 57% of developers reporting agents in production
